Daytime streetlights spark public fund misuse concerns amid Guwahati's power woes
As electricity demand rises, city residents have sought greater accountability while civic agencies maintain the lights were switched on for maintenance purposes
Guwahati, June 2: At a time when many parts of Guwahati are struggling with power-related issues during the ongoing heatwave, citizens have raised their eyebrows after spotting streetlights functioning during broad daylight in different parts of the city.
The issue was noticed in several parts of the city including Ganeshguri and RG Baruah Road, where streetlights remained switched on despite bright daylight conditions. The sight has drawn criticism from citizens who questioned the apparent wastage of electricity and public funds.
Speaking to The Assam Tribune, a daily commuter in Ganeshguri said, “These streetlights are functioning in broad daylight which is difficult to understand when many residents are already facing electricity-related problems. In Kahilipara, where I live, the power has been fluctuating since morning, causing inconvenience to households and people working from home.”
